title: The Big Picture: Supramax market
summary:
Main
By Mark Nugent

Holding steady
The Supramaxes now receive a premium over all other sectors as several trades for these vessels see fresh demand. We look at where the new demand is coming from. Shipments bounce back in March
The Baltic Supramax 58 average today settled at $29,105/day, marking a 24.6% increase YoY, but a 9.5% decline MoM following a broad decline in dry bulk rates from the end of March.

children.title: Holding steady
children.summary: The Supramaxes now receive a premium over all other sectors as several trades for these vessels see fresh demand. We look at where the new demand is coming from.

children.title: Shipments bounce back in March
children.summary: The Baltic Supramax 58 average today settled at $29,105/day, marking a 24.6% increase YoY, but a 9.5% decline MoM following a broad decline in dry bulk rates from the end of March. Total liftings on Supramaxes in March totalled 98m tonnes, declining by 4.4% YoY, although the highest level since June 2021.

children.title: European Supramax trade robust
children.summary: In March, dry bulk shipments imported into Europe on Supramaxes totalled 7.3m tonnes, increasing by 13.8% YoY. Average voyage lengths on Supramax voyages into Europe, increased by over 6 days in March YoY, in part due to a speed reduction owing to high bunker prices.

children.title: Tight tonnage supply in USG
children.summary: Rates out of the US Gulf have emerged as the strongest performer among the Baltic-assessed Supramax routes in the past week. Largely down to a lack of tonnage availability in the region, we count a total of 28 Supramax vessels ballasting towards the US that also discharged in April.

children.title: Petcoke providing new demand
children.summary: Supramax demand generated from petroleum coke, otherwise known as petcoke, hit new highs in March, rising by 85.2% YoY. Shipments of petcoke on Supramaxes totalled 3.1m tonnes in March, the highest on record, increasing by 57.7% YoY.

children.title: India congestion
children.summary: Supramax congestion in India has risen 87.5% WoW to 797k dwt, currently more than double the 5-year average. This is likely as a result of growing interest in Indian grains, which the country has pledged to ship more of following the Ukraine war.
